subject: Accounting
title: Debt-to-Equity Ratio
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Debt-to-Equity Ratio.

hook.text: A company with $3 of debt for every $1 of equity is living dangerously. One bad quarter and it's over.

buildup.text: The debt-to-equity ratio compares total liabilities to shareholders' equity.

discovery.text: It reveals how much of the business is funded by debt versus owners' money.

twist.text: Some industries like banking naturally carry high ratios. Context is everything.

climax.text: A rising ratio over time means the company is increasingly relying on borrowed money.

punchline.text: More debt, more risk. The ratio tells the story.

quiz.question: What does a high debt-to-equity ratio indicate?
